content: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) can be reframed as a standardized inter-agent memory communication protocol that unifies access to both short-term and long-term agent memory systems across disparate AI agents and tools. Instead of only connecting AI tools to external data sources, MCP could enable cross-agent sharing of segmented memory: agents can securely pull context-relevant fragments from another agent’s short-term working memory or retrieve compressed, persisted insights from its long-term memory without requiring full memory duplication or proprietary format conversion.
